question: Which imaging modality in the Multi-Spectral Stereo Dataset was specifically evaluated using the Monocular Depth Estimation Model?

options.A: Thermal imaging, as it is a common type used extensively in multi-spectral stereo datasets.
options.B: Near-Infrared (NIR), as it is a type of imaging modality included in the dataset for evaluation.
options.C: Ultraviolet (UV) imaging, as it is often included in spectral studies for depth estimation.
options.D: RGB imaging, as it is the standard visual spectrum used in most imaging studies.

explanation: The Monocular Depth Estimation Model was evaluated on a Multi-Spectral Stereo Dataset that includes NIR. NIR (Near-Infrared) is specifically mentioned as a type of imaging modality included in the dataset. This aligns with the knowledge graph path from the model to the dataset and then to NIR as a type of Imaging Modality.
